description: Interpret the product of a fraction multiplied by a fraction (a/b) × (c/d). Use a visual fraction model and create a story context for this equation.
description_plain: Your child can understand what it means to multiply one fraction by another using pictures and stories.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the product (a/b) × q as a parts of a partition of q into b equal parts.
description_plain: Your child can understand multiplying a fraction by a whole number as taking parts of that whole number divided into equal pieces.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction by a whole number and a fraction by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply fractions by whole numbers and by other fractions using what they already know about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as the number that results from dividing the whole number numerator by the whole number denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands a fraction as the result of dividing the top number by the bottom number and can solve word problems that lead to answers in fractions or mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about multiplication and division to work with fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators by using a variety of representations, equations, and visual models to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers (e.g. recognize an incorrect result 2/5 + ½ = 3/7, by observing that 3/7 < ½).
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add or subtract fractions with different bottom numbers by using pictures or equations and can estimate answers to check if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators (e.g., 2/3 + 5/4 = 8/12 + 15/12 = 23/12).
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different bottom numbers (including mixed numbers) by changing them to equivalent fractions with the same bottom number.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child uses equivalent fractions to add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, connecting objects or drawings to strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between operations. Relate the strategy to a written form.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths using drawings or objects and explain their methods.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend understanding of division to find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors.
description_plain: Your child can divide whole numbers with up to four digits by two-digit numbers and find the whole number answers.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using a standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can multiply multi-digit whole numbers quickly and correctly using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can work with large whole numbers and decimals to the hundredths place using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round decimals to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to the thousandths place using >, =, and < symbols.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals to the thousandths place in numbers, words, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and compare decimals up to the thousandths place.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10, and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10.
description_plain: Your child can explain patterns when multiplying or dividing by powers of 10, like how zeros or decimal points move, and use exponents to show powers of 10.

subject: Math
description: Apply concepts of place value, multiplication, and division to understand that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a multi-digit number, each digit is 10 times bigger than the digit to its right and one-tenth the size of the digit to its left.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how place value works in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand primes have only two factors and decompose numbers into prime factors.
description_plain: Your child understands that prime numbers have only two factors and can break numbers down into their prime factors.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules (e.g., generate terms in the resulting sequences). Identify and explain the apparent relationships between corresponding terms. Form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns, and graph the ordered pairs on a coordinate plane (e.g., given the rule "add 3" and the starting number 0, and given the rule "add 6" and the starting number 0, generate terms in the resulting sequences, and observe that the terms in one sequence are twice the corresponding terms in the other sequence).
description_plain: Your child can create two number patterns from given rules, find relationships between the patterns, make pairs from matching terms, and graph these pairs on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find and understand patterns and relationships in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers, and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them (e.g., express the calculation "add 8 and 7, then multiply by 2" as 2 × (8 + 7). Recognize that 3 × (18,932 + 921) is three times as large as 18,932 + 921, without having to calculate the indicated sum or product).
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what these expressions mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Use parentheses and brackets in numerical expressions, and evaluate expressions with these symbols (Order of Operations).
description_plain: Your child can use parentheses and brackets to organize math expressions and correctly solve them by following the order of operations.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write and understand math expressions.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child notices patterns when solving problems and uses these patterns to help find solutions.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child looks for and uses the structure or parts of a problem to solve it more easily.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses the right tools, like calculators or rulers, to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking clearly and listens to others to understand and improve solutions.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child thinks about math problems both with numbers and ideas to understand and solve them.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child understands math problems and keeps trying different ways to solve them until they find an answer.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model (which may not be uniform) by observing frequencies in data generated from a chance process.
description_plain: Your child creates a probability model based on how often things happen in real-life chance events.

subject: Math
description: Develop a uniform probability model by assigning equal probability to all outcomes, and use the model to determine probabilities of events.
description_plain: Your child makes a simple probability model where all outcomes are equally likely and uses it to find chances of events.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model and use it to find probabilities of events. Compare probabilities from a model to observed frequencies. If the agreement is not good, explain possible sources of the discrepancy.
description_plain: Your child builds probability models to find chances of events and compares these to actual results, explaining differences.

subject: Math
description: Approximate the probability of a chance event by collecting data on the chance process that produces it and observing its long-run relative frequency, and predict the approximate relative frequency given the probability.
description_plain: Your child estimates the chance of an event by collecting data and watching how often it happens over time.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the probability of a chance event is a number between 0 and 1 that expresses the likelihood of the event occurring. Larger numbers indicate greater likelihood. A probability near 0 indicates an unlikely event, a probability around 1/2 indicates an event that is neither unlikely nor likely, and a probability near 1 indicates a likely event.
description_plain: Your child understands that probability is a number from 0 to 1 showing how likely something is to happen, with bigger numbers meaning more likely.

subject: Math
description: Investigate chance processes and develop, use and evaluate probability models.
description_plain: Your child explores chance events and creates, uses, and checks probability models.

subject: Math
description: Use measures of center and measures of variability for numerical data from random samples to draw informal comparative inferences about two populations.
description_plain: Your child uses averages and variation from random samples to compare two groups and make guesses about them.

subject: Math
description: Informally assess the degree of visual overlap of two numerical data distributions with similar variabilities, measuring the difference between the centers by expressing it as a multiple of a measure of variability.
description_plain: Your child looks at two sets of data to see how much they overlap and compares their centers using measures of spread.

subject: Math
description: Draw informal comparative inferences about two populations.
description_plain: Your child makes informal comparisons between two groups using data.

subject: Math
description: Use data from a random sample to draw inferences about a population with an unknown characteristic of interest. Generate multiple samples (or simulated samples) of the same size to gauge the variation in estimates or predictions.
description_plain: Your child uses data from random samples to make guesses about a larger group and tries multiple samples to see how results can change.

subject: Math
description: Understand that statistics can be used to gain information about a population by examining a sample of the population; generalizations about a population from a sample are valid only if the sample is representative of that population. Understand that random sampling tends to produce representative samples and support valid inferences.
description_plain: Your child understands that looking at a small, random part of a group can give good information about the whole group if the sample is fair.

subject: Math
description: Use random sampling to draw inferences about a population.
description_plain: Your child uses random samples to learn about larger groups.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems and problems in a real-world context involving area, of two-dimensional objects composed of triangles, quadrilaterals, and other polygons. Solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context involving volume and surface area of three-dimensional objects composed of cubes and right prisms.
description_plain: Your child solves problems about the area of flat shapes made of triangles and other polygons, and about the volume and surface area of 3D shapes like cubes and prisms.

subject: Math
description: Use facts about supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles in multi-step problems to write and solve simple equations for an unknown angle in a figure.
description_plain: Your child uses facts about different types of angles to write and solve simple equations to find unknown angles in shapes.

subject: Math
description: Understand and use the formulas for the area and circumference of a circle to solve problems; give an informal derivation of the relationship between the circumference and area of a circle.
description_plain: Your child uses formulas for the area and circumference of circles to solve problems and understands how these formulas are related.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context involving angle measure, area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child solves problems involving angles, area, surface area, and volume in math and real life.

subject: Math
description: Describe the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing three-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child describes the flat shapes that appear when slicing through 3D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Draw geometric shapes with given conditions using a variety of methods. Focus on constructing triangles from three measures of angles or sides, noticing when the conditions determine a unique triangle, more than one triangle, or no triangle.
description_plain: Your child draws shapes with given side lengths or angles and figures out if these measurements make one, more than one, or no triangles.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures, such as computing actual lengths and areas from a scale drawing and reproducing a scale drawing at a different scale.
description_plain: Your child solves problems using scale drawings by finding actual lengths and areas or making drawings at different scales.

subject: Math
description: Draw, construct, and describe geometrical figures, and describe the relationships between them.
description_plain: Your child draws and builds geometric shapes and explains how they relate to each other.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems leading to inequalities of the form px + q > r or px + q < r, where p, q, and r are rational numbers. Graph the solution set of the inequality and interpret it in the context of the problem.
description_plain: Your child solves word problems that lead to inequalities, graphs the solutions, and explains what the answers mean.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems leading to equations of the form px + q = r and p(x + q) = r, where p, q, and r are specific rational numbers. Solve equations of these forms fluently. Compare an algebraic solution to an arithmetic solution, identifying the sequence of the operations used in each approach.
description_plain: Your child solves word problems that lead to equations, solves them easily, and compares algebraic and arithmetic methods.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent quantities in mathematical problems and problems in real-world context, and construct simple equations and inequalities to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child uses letters to stand for numbers in problems and writes simple equations and inequalities to solve them.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step mathematical problems and problems in real-world context posed with positive and negative rational numbers in any form. Convert between forms as appropriate and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child solves multi-step problems using positive and negative numbers in different forms and checks if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context using numerical and algebraic expressions and equations.
description_plain: Your child solves math and real-life problems using numbers, expressions, and equations.

subject: Math
description: Rewrite an expression in different forms, and understand the relationship between the different forms and their meanings in a problem context.
description_plain: Your child rewrites math expressions in different ways and understands how these forms relate to the problem.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add, subtract, factor, and expand linear expressions with rational coefficients.
description_plain: Your child uses math rules to add, subtract, factor, and expand expressions with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child uses math properties to create expressions that mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context involving the four operations with rational numbers. Computations with rational numbers extend the rules for manipulating fractions to complex fractions where a/b ÷ c/d when a, b, c, and d are all integers and b, c, and d ≠ 0.
description_plain: Your child solves problems using all four operations with rational numbers, including complex fractions.

subject: Math
description: Convert a rational number to decimal form using long division; know that the decimal form of a rational number terminates in 0's or eventually repeats.
description_plain: Your child changes rational numbers into decimals using long division and knows decimals either end or repeat.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply and divide rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child uses math rules to multiply and divide rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that integers can be divided, provided that the divisor is not zero, and every quotient of integers (with non-zero divisor) is a rational number. If p and q are integers, then –(p/q) = (–p)/q = p/(–q). Interpret quotients of rational numbers by describing real-world context.
description_plain: Your child understands dividing integers works when the divisor isn't zero and explains division of rational numbers using real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Understand that multiplication is extended from fractions to rational numbers by requiring that operations continue to satisfy the properties of operations, particularly the distributive property, leading to products such as (–1)(–1) = 1 and the rules for multiplying signed numbers. Interpret products of rational numbers by describing real-world context.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplying rational numbers follows math rules, including multiplying negatives, and explains these products with real-world examples.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide integers and other rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide whole numbers, fractions, and decimals, including negative numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add and subtract rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about adding and subtracting numbers to help solve problems with fractions and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Understand subtraction of rational numbers as adding the additive inverse, p – q = p + (–q). Show that the distance between two rational numbers on the number line is the absolute value of their difference, and apply this principle in real-world context.
description_plain: Your child understands that subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite and can find the distance between two numbers on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand p + q as the number located a distance |q| from p, in the positive or negative direction depending on whether q is positive or negative. Show that a number and its opposite have a sum of 0 (are additive inverses). Interpret sums of rational numbers by describing real-world context.
description_plain: Your child understands adding positive or negative numbers as moving along a number line and knows that a number plus its opposite equals zero.

subject: Math
description: Describe situations in which opposite quantities combine to make 0.
description_plain: Your child can explain situations where two opposite amounts combine to make zero.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract integers and other rational numbers; represent addition and subtraction on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract whole numbers, fractions, and decimals, and show these operations on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understanding of operations with fractions to add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers except division by zero.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions and decimals, except dividing by zero.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional relationships to solve multi-step ratio and percent problems (e.g., simple interest, tax, markups and markdowns, gratuities and commissions, fees, percent increase and decrease, percent error).
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step problems using ratios and percentages, like calculating tax, tips, discounts, and interest.

subject: Math
description: Explain what a point (x, y) on the graph of a proportional relationship means in terms of the situation, with special attention to the points (0, 0) and (1, r) where r is the unit rate.
description_plain: Your child can explain what points on a graph mean in a proportional relationship, especially the starting point and the unit rate.

subject: Math
description: Represent proportional relationships by equations.
description_plain: Your child can write equations that show proportional relationships between quantities.

subject: Math
description: Identify the constant of proportionality (unit rate) in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and verbal descriptions of proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find the unit rate (constant ratio) in tables, graphs, equations, pictures, and words.

subject: Math
description: Decide whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship (e.g., by testing for equivalent ratios in a table or graphing on a coordinate plane and observing whether the graph is a straight line through the origin).
description_plain: Your child can tell if two quantities are proportional by checking if their ratios are equal or if their graph is a straight line through zero.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent proportional relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and show proportional relationships between amounts.

subject: Math
description: Compute unit rates associated with ratios involving both simple and complex fractions, including ratios of quantities measured in like or different units.
description_plain: Your child can find unit rates for ratios, even when the numbers are fractions or use different units.

subject: Math
description: Analyze proportional relationships and use them to solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use proportional relationships to solve math and real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns when solving problems repeatedly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the structure or pattern in math problems to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like rulers or calculators, to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking clearly and understand others’ math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems both with numbers and in real-world terms.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand math problems and keep trying until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Design and use a simulation to generate frequencies for compound events.
description_plain: Your child can create and use simulations to find how often combined events happen.

subject: Math
description: Represent sample spaces for compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams and other methods. Identify the outcomes in the sample space which compose the event.
description_plain: Your child can list all possible outcomes for combined events using tables, lists, or tree diagrams and pick the ones that match a specific event.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the probability of a compound event is the fraction of outcomes in the sample space for which the compound event occurs.
description_plain: Your child understands that the chance of combined events is the fraction of all possible outcomes where those events happen.

subject: Math
description: Find probabilities of compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulation.
description_plain: Your child can find the probability of combined events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, or simulations.

subject: Math
description: Investigate chance processes and develop, use, and evaluate probability models.
description_plain: Your child can explore chance events and create and check models that show how likely things are to happen.

subject: Math
description: Understand that patterns of association can also be seen in bivariate categorical data by displaying frequencies and relative frequencies in a two-way table. Construct and interpret a two-way table summarizing data on two categorical variables collected from the same subjects. Use relative frequencies calculated for rows or columns to describe possible association between the two variables.
description_plain: Your child can make and read two-way tables to see how two categories of data might be related.

subject: Math
description: Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems in the context of bivariate measurement data, interpreting the slope and intercept.
description_plain: Your child can use a line equation to solve problems with two related measurements and explain what the slope and intercept mean.

subject: Math
description: Know that straight lines are widely used to model relationships between two quantitative variables. For scatter plots that suggest a linear association, informally fit a straight line, and informally assess the model fit by judging the closeness of the data points to the line.
description_plain: Your child knows that straight lines often show relationships between two numbers and can draw a line that fits data points on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate and describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.
description_plain: Your child can make and understand scatter plots to find patterns like groups, outliers, or trends between two measurements.

subject: Math
description: Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data.
description_plain: Your child can study how two sets of data relate to each other.

subject: Math
description: Understand and use formulas for volumes of cones, cylinders and spheres and use them to solve real-world context and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of cones, cylinders, and spheres and solve related problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems involving the volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points in a coordinate system.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles in real-world context and mathematical problems in two and three dimensions.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find missing side lengths in right triangles in both flat and 3D problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand the Pythagorean Theorem and its converse.
description_plain: Your child understands the Pythagorean Theorem and knows how to use it both ways.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply the Pythagorean Theorem.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use the Pythagorean Theorem to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use informal arguments to establish facts about the angle sum and exterior angle of triangles, about the angles created when parallel lines are cut by a transversal, and the angle-angle criterion for similarity of triangles.
description_plain: Your child can explain facts about angles in triangles and parallel lines and how to tell if triangles are similar.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is similar to another if, and only if, one can be obtained from the other by a sequence of rotations, reflections, translations, and dilations; given two similar two-dimensional figures, describe a sequence that demonstrates similarity.
description_plain: Your child understands that two shapes are similar if one can be changed into the other by flipping, sliding, turning, or resizing, and can describe how to do this.

subject: Math
description: Describe the effect of dilations, translations, rotations, and reflections on two-dimensional figures using coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can describe how moving, flipping, turning, or resizing shapes changes their position using coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is congruent to another if one can be obtained from the other by a sequence of rotations, reflections, and translations; given two congruent figures, describe a sequence that demonstrates congruence.
description_plain: Your child can tell when two flat shapes are exactly the same by turning, flipping, or sliding one to match the other, and explain the steps they used.

subject: Math
description: Verify experimentally the properties of rotations, reflections, and translations. Properties include: lines are taken to lines, line segments are taken to line segments of the same length, angles are taken to angles of the same measure, parallel lines are taken to parallel lines.
description_plain: Your child can test and show how turning, flipping, and sliding shapes keep lines straight, keep lengths and angles the same, and keep parallel lines parallel.

subject: Math
description: Understand congruence and similarity.
description_plain: Your child understands when shapes are the same size and shape (congruent) and when they have the same shape but different sizes (similar).

subject: Math
description: Describe qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities by analyzing a graph (e.g., where the function is increasing or decreasing, linear or nonlinear). Sketch a graph that exhibits the qualitative features of a function that has been described verbally.
description_plain: Your child can look at a graph and describe how two things change together, like when a line goes up or down, and draw a graph from a description.

subject: Math
description: Given a description of a situation, generate a function to model a linear relationship between two quantities. Determine the rate of change and initial value of the function from a description of a relationship or from two (x, y) values, including reading these from a table or a graph. Track how the values of the two quantities change together. Interpret the rate of change and initial value of a linear function in terms of the situation it models, its graph, or its table of values.
description_plain: Your child can create a rule (function) to show how two things are related in a straight-line way, find how fast one changes compared to the other, and explain what these numbers mean.

subject: Math
description: Use functions to model relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child uses functions to show how different amounts are connected.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the equation y = mx + b as defining a linear function whose graph is a straight line; give examples of functions that are not linear.
description_plain: Your child understands that the equation y = mx + b makes a straight line on a graph and can give examples of rules that don’t make straight lines.

subject: Math
description: Compare properties of two functions each represented in a different way (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, or by verbal descriptions).
description_plain: Your child can compare two functions shown in different ways, like with an equation, graph, table, or words.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a function is a rule that assigns to each input exactly one output. The graph of a function is the set of ordered pairs consisting of an input and the corresponding output. (Function notation is not required in Grade 8.)
description_plain: Your child understands that a function is a rule that gives exactly one answer for each input, and can find points on its graph that match inputs and outputs.

subject: Math
description: Define, evaluate, and compare functions.
description_plain: Your child can define what functions are, find their values, and compare different functions.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context leading to two linear equations in two variables.
description_plain: Your child can solve math and real-life problems that involve two straight-line equations with two unknowns.

subject: Math
description: Solve systems of two linear equations in two variables algebraically, and estimate solutions by graphing the equations including cases of no solution and infinite number of solutions. Solve simple cases by inspection.
description_plain: Your child can solve two straight-line equations with two unknowns using algebra, estimate answers by graphing, and solve simple cases by looking.

subject: Math
description: Understand that solutions to a system of two linear equations in two variables correspond to points of intersection of their graphs, because points of intersection satisfy both equations simultaneously.
description_plain: Your child understands that the solution to two straight-line equations is where their graphs cross, because that point fits both equations.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve pairs of simultaneous linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can analyze and solve pairs of straight-line equations at the same time.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations and inequalities with rational number coefficients, including solutions that require expanding expressions using the distributive property and collecting like terms.
description_plain: Your child can solve straight-line equations and inequalities with fractions and decimals, including when they need to expand and combine terms.

subject: Math
description: Give examples of linear equations in one variable with one solution, infinitely many solutions, or no solution. Show which of these possibilities is the case by successively transforming the given equation into simpler forms, until an equivalent equation of the form x = a, a = a, or a = b results (where a and b are different numbers).
description_plain: Your child can give examples of straight-line equations with one solution, many solutions, or no solutions, and show which by simplifying the equation step-by-step.

subject: Math
description: Fluently solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately solve straight-line equations and inequalities with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve linear equations, inequalities, and pairs of simultaneous linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can analyze and solve straight-line equations, inequalities, and pairs of straight-line equations.

subject: Math
description: Use similar triangles to explain why the slope m is the same between any two distinct points on a non-vertical line in the coordinate plane. Derive the equation y = mx for a line through the origin and the equation y = mx + b for a line intercepting the vertical axis at (0, b).
description_plain: Your child can explain why the slope (steepness) is the same between any two points on a line and use the equations y = mx and y = mx + b to describe lines.

subject: Math
description: Graph proportional relationships interpreting the unit rate as the slope of the graph. Compare two different proportional relationships represented in different ways.
description_plain: Your child can graph proportional relationships, understand the unit rate as the slope, and compare two different proportional relationships shown in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Understand the connections between proportional relationships, lines, and linear equations.
description_plain: Your child understands how proportional relationships, lines, and straight-line equations are connected.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with numbers expressed in scientific notation including problems where both decimal and scientific notation are used. Use scientific notation and choose units of appropriate size for measurements of very large or very small quantities.
description_plain: Your child can do math with very big or very small numbers written in scientific notation and choose the right units for measurements.

subject: Math
description: Use numbers expressed in the form of a single digit times an integer power of 10 to estimate very large or very small quantities, and express how many times larger or smaller one is than the other.
description_plain: Your child can use numbers written as a digit times a power of 10 to estimate very big or small amounts and compare how many times bigger one is than another.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate cube roots of perfect cubes less than or equal to 1000.
description_plain: Your child can find cube roots of perfect cubes up to 1000.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate square roots of perfect squares less than or equal to 225.
description_plain: Your child can find square roots of perfect squares up to 225.

subject: Math
description: Use square root and cube root symbols to represent solutions to equations of the form x² = p and x³= p, where p is a positive rational number. Know that √2 is irrational.
description_plain: Your child can use square root and cube root symbols to solve equations like x² = p and x³ = p, and knows that √2 is not a simple fraction.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply the properties of integer exponents to generate equivalent numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses the rules for exponents with whole numbers to rewrite expressions in different but equal ways.

subject: Math
description: Work with radicals and integer exponents.
description_plain: Your child works with roots (like square roots) and whole number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Understand that given any two distinct rational numbers, a < b, there exist a rational number c and an irrational number d such that a < c < b and a < d < b. Given any two distinct irrational numbers, a < b, there exist a rational number c and an irrational number d such that a < c < b and a < d < b.
description_plain: Your child understands that between any two different rational or irrational numbers, there are other rational and irrational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use rational approximations of irrational numbers to compare the size of irrational numbers. Locate them approximately on a number line diagram, and estimate their values.
description_plain: Your child can use close rational numbers to compare and estimate the size and position of irrational numbers on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Know that numbers that are not rational are called irrational. Understand informally that every number has a decimal expansion. Know that numbers whose decimal expansions do not terminate in zeros or in a repeating sequence of fixed digits are called irrational.
description_plain: Your child knows that numbers that can’t be written as fractions are called irrational, understands that all numbers have decimal forms, and can change repeating decimals back into fractions.

subject: Math
description: Understand that there are irrational numbers, and approximate them using rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands that irrational numbers exist and can approximate them using rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and repeated ideas when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child notices and uses the way things are organized or structured to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses the best tools to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their thinking clearly and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child thinks about math problems both in general ideas and with actual numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child understands problems and keeps trying different ways to solve them.

subject: Math
description: Relating the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right way to describe data based on how the data looks and where it came from.

subject: Math
description: Giving quantitative measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (interquartile range and/or mean absolute deviation), as well as describing any overall pattern and any striking deviations from the overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the middle and spread of data, describe overall patterns, and point out anything unusual, using the context of the data.

subject: Math
description: Describing the nature of the attribute under investigation including how it was measured and its units of measurement.
description_plain: Your child can explain what is being measured in a data set, how it was measured, and what units were used.

subject: Math
description: Reporting the number of observations.
description_plain: Your child can tell how many observations or data points were collected.

subject: Math
description: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context by:
description_plain: Your child can summarize number data by explaining what the numbers mean in their real-life context.

subject: Math
description: Display and interpret numerical data by creating plots on a number line including histograms, dot plots, and box plots.
description_plain: Your child can create and understand different types of graphs like histograms, dot plots, and box plots on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Summarize and describe distributions.
description_plain: Your child can summarize and describe how data is spread out and what patterns it shows.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a measure of center for a numerical data set summarizes all of its values with a single number, while a measure of variation uses a single number to describe the spread of the data set.
description_plain: Your child understands that one number can show the middle of data, and another number can show how much the data varies.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a set of data collected to answer a statistical question has a distribution whose general characteristics can be described by its center, spread, and overall shape.
description_plain: Your child understands that data has a center, spread, and shape that describe its overall pattern.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for variability in the answers.
description_plain: Your child can recognize questions that expect different answers because the data changes each time.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of statistical variability.
description_plain: Your child understands that data can vary and learns how to describe that variability.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles, and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures. Apply these techniques to solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns (nets) made of rectangles and triangles to build 3D shapes and use these to find surface area in real problems.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices; use coordinates to find the length of a side joining points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate. Apply these techniques to solve mathematical problems and problems in a real-world context.
description_plain: Your child can draw shapes on a grid using points and find side lengths when sides are straight up/down or left/right, solving real problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with fractional edge lengths by packing it with unit cubes of the appropriate unit fraction edge lengths, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths of the prism. Understand and use the formula V = B · h, where in this case, B is the area of the base (B = l × w) to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths in mathematical problems and problems in real-world context.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of boxes with fractional side lengths by filling them with small cubes and by multiplying the edges, using formulas in real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special quadrilaterals, and polygons by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and other shapes; apply these techniques to solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of triangles, special four-sided shapes, and other polygons by breaking them into rectangles or triangles, and use this in real problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context involving area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child can solve math and real-world problems involving area, surface area, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent two quantities that change in relationship to one another to solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context. Write an equation to express one quantity (the dependent variable) in terms of the other quantity (the independent variable). Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation.
description_plain: Your child can use variables to show how two things change together, write equations to connect them, and use graphs and tables to understand their relationship.

subject: Math
description: Represent and analyze quantitative relationships between dependent and independent variables.
description_plain: Your child can show and study how one quantity depends on another using math.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x > c, x < c, x ≥ c, or x ≤ c to represent a constraint or condition to solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context. Recognize that inequalities have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of such inequalities on number lines.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities like x > 5 to show limits or conditions, solve them, and show all solutions on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context by writing and solving equations of the form x + p = q, x - p = q, px = q, and x/p = q for cases in which p, q and x are all non-negative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple equations like x + 3 = 7 or 4x = 12 when numbers are not negative.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent numbers and write expressions when solving mathematical problems and problems in real-world context; understand that a variable can represent an unknown number or any number in a specified set.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers and write expressions to solve math and real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand solving an equation or inequality as a process of reasoning to find the value(s) of the variables that make that equation or inequality true. Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.
description_plain: Your child understands solving equations or inequalities means finding numbers that make them true and can check answers by plugging in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Reason about and solve one-variable equations and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child can think through and solve equations and inequalities with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent.
description_plain: Your child can tell when two math expressions mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to create different expressions that are equal.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate expressions given specific values of their variables. Include expressions that arise from formulas used to solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context. Perform arithmetic operations, including those involving whole-number exponents, in the conventional order when there are no parentheses to specify a particular order (Order of Operations).
description_plain: Your child can calculate the value of expressions when given numbers for the variables, following the correct order of operations.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms (sum, term, product, factor, quotient, and coefficient); view one or more parts of an expression as a single entity.
description_plain: Your child can name parts of an expression like sum, product, factor, and coefficient, and see parts as a whole when needed.

subject: Math
description: Write expressions that record operations with numbers and variables.
description_plain: Your child can write expressions that show math operations with numbers and variables.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and find the value of algebraic expressions.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve number expressions that include whole-number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understanding of arithmetic to algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about arithmetic to understand and work with algebra expressions.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on all four parts of a coordinate grid and use coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order in mathematical problems and problems in real-world context.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between comparing absolute values and comparing order of numbers in math and real life.

subject: Math
description: Understand the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line; interpret absolute value as magnitude for a positive or negative quantity in real-world context.
description_plain: Your child understands absolute value as the distance of a number from zero and can explain it in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real-world context.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain statements about the order of rational numbers in real-life contexts.

subject: Math
description: Interpret statements of inequality as statements about the relative position of two numbers on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can understand inequalities as showing which number is bigger or smaller on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand ordering and absolute value of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how to order rational numbers and what absolute value means.

subject: Math
description: Find and position integers and other rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram; find and position pairs of integers and other rational numbers on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and place integers and other rational numbers on number lines and coordinate planes.

subject: Math
description: Understand signs of numbers in ordered pairs as indicating locations in quadrants of the coordinate plane; recognize that when two ordered pairs differ only by signs, the locations of the points are related by reflections across one or both axes.
description_plain: Your child understands that the signs in ordered pairs tell where points are on the coordinate plane and that changing signs reflects points across axes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize opposite signs of numbers as indicating locations on opposite sides of 0 on the number line; recognize that the opposite of the opposite of a number is the number itself and that 0 is its own opposite.
description_plain: Your child knows that opposite signs mean numbers are on opposite sides of zero and that the opposite of the opposite number is the original number.

subject: Math
description: Understand a rational number can be represented as a point on the number line. Extend number line diagrams and coordinate axes familiar from previous grades to represent points on the line and in the plane with negative number coordinates.
description_plain: Your child understands that rational numbers can be shown as points on number lines and coordinate planes, including negative numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values. Use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in real-world context, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation.
description_plain: Your child understands that positive and negative numbers can show things like direction or value, and can explain what zero means in different situations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understanding of numbers to the system of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about numbers to understand and work with all rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use the distributive property to express a sum of two whole numbers 1 to 100 with a common factor as a multiple of a sum of two whole numbers with no common factor. For example, express 36 + 8 as 4(9+2).
description_plain: Your child can rewrite a sum of two numbers that share a common factor as a multiplication of that factor times a simpler sum, like changing 36 + 8 into 4 × (9 + 2).

subject: Math
description: Find the least common multiple of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12.
description_plain: Your child can find the smallest number that two numbers (up to 12) both divide into evenly.

subject: Math
description: Find the greatest common factor of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100.
description_plain: Your child can find the biggest number that divides two numbers (up to 100) without leaving a remainder.

subject: Math
description: Use previous understanding of factors to find the greatest common factor and the least common multiple.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about factors to find both the greatest common factor and the least common multiple of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit decimals using a standard algorithm for each operation.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals with many digits using standard methods.

subject: Math
description: Fluently divide multi-digit numbers using a standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly divide large numbers using the usual step-by-step method.

subject: Math
description: Compute fluently with multi-digit numbers and find common factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child can work easily with large numbers and find common factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and compute quotients of fractions to solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context involving division of fractions by fractions using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can understand and solve problems that involve dividing fractions by fractions, using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understanding of multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.
description_plain: Your child builds on what they know about multiplication and division to divide fractions by other fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio reasoning to convert measurement units; manipulate and transform units appropriately when multiplying or dividing quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use ratio thinking to change measurement units and correctly adjust units when multiplying or dividing.

subject: Math
description: Find a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100 (e.g., 30% of a quantity means 30/100 times the quantity). Solve percent problems with the unknown in all positions of the equation.
description_plain: Your child can find a percent of a number and solve problems where they know a part and the percent to find the whole amount.

subject: Math
description: Solve unit rate problems including those involving unit pricing and constant speed.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about unit rates, like finding the best price per item or speed.

subject: Math
description: Make tables of equivalent ratios relating quantities with whole-number measurements, find missing values in the tables, and plot the pairs of values on the coordinate plane. Use tables to compare ratios.
description_plain: Your child can make tables showing equivalent ratios with whole numbers, find missing numbers, and graph these pairs to compare ratios.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve mathematical problems and problems in real-world context (e.g., by reasoning about data collected from measurements, tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, or equations).
description_plain: Your child uses ratio and rate thinking to solve math and real-life problems using tools like tables, diagrams, and equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a unit rate a/b associated with a ratio a : b with b ≠ 0, and use rate language (e.g., for every, for each, for each 1, per) in the context of a ratio relationship. (Complex fraction notation is not an expectation for unit rates in this grade level.)
description_plain: Your child understands what a unit rate is and uses words like 'for every' or 'per' to describe how quantities compare.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a ratio as comparing two quantities multiplicatively or joining/composing the two quantities in a way that preserves a multiplicative relationship. Use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities.
description_plain: Your child understands a ratio as a way to compare two amounts by multiplication and can explain the relationship using ratio words.

subject: Math
description: Understand ratio concepts and use ratio reasoning to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child understands ratios and can use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child notices and explains patterns that happen again and again when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child notices and explains patterns that happen again and again when solving problems.

description_plain: Your child looks for and uses the way things are organized or structured to help solve problems.

description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking clearly and understand other people's ideas about math.

description_plain: Your child tries to understand math problems and keeps working on them until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Apply geometric methods to solve design problems utilizing real-world context.
description_plain: Your child uses geometry to solve design problems that relate to real-life situations.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child uses geometry to solve design problems that relate to real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations utilizing real-world context.
description_plain: Your child uses ideas about how dense things are based on area and volume to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations utilizing real-world context.
description_plain: Your child uses ideas about how dense things are based on area and volume to solve real-world problems.

description_plain: Your child uses ideas about how dense things are based on area and volume to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe objects utilizing real-world context.
description_plain: Your child uses shapes and their measurements to describe real objects.

subject: Math
description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe objects utilizing real-world context.
description_plain: Your child uses shapes and their measurements to describe real objects.
