subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new grade-level words, including subject-specific terms, and look up meanings when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings or associations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, religious, and mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech and references in stories by looking at the context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check to make sure they have found the correct meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the surrounding words and sentence meaning to figure out the meaning of unknown words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin word parts to figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases in their reading by using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child will choose words that clearly and simply express their ideas, avoiding extra or repeated words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use language rules correctly when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct spelling.
description_plain: Use correct spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives.
description_plain: Your child will use a comma to separate two adjectives that both describe the same noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child will use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can put phrases and clauses in the right place in a sentence and fix sentences where they are in the wrong spot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child will choose different types of sentences (simple, compound, complex) to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what phrases and clauses do in a sentence and how they work in specific examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child will use proper grammar and word usage when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, using formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other visuals in presentations to help explain their ideas and highlight important points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, appropriate vocabulary, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share ideas and important details when speaking, using good eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument and claims, and judge if the reasons and evidence make sense and are strong.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, and orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand main ideas and details from different types of media and explain how they help explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new information from others and change their own opinions when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that encourage others to explain more, and respond to others with comments that keep the discussion on track.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow discussion rules, keep track of goals and deadlines, and help define roles in group work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to think about and talk about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions on 7th grade topics, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, both for longer projects that take time and for shorter assignments, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literary nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child applies reading skills to understand nonfiction writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literature.
description_plain: Your child applies reading skills to understand stories and literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thoughts and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer a question, using several sources and coming up with new, related questions to explore.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses technology like the internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child plans and improves their writing by revising and editing to better fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense and reflects on the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and descriptive words to show action and share experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop the story and its characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that introduce characters and events in a clear and logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and a well-organized sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes an ending that fits and supports the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain the topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transitions to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child explains their topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces the topic, organizes information in a logical way, and uses things like headings, graphics, or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that explain a topic clearly by choosing, organizing, and analyzing the right information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes an ending that fits and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect their claim, reasons, and evidence so the writing is clear and organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their argument with logical reasons and evidence from reliable sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main argument, mention other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their ideas with clear reasons and evidence that makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend informational texts and nonfiction in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 7.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level nonfiction texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different authors write about the same topic, noticing what evidence they use and how they interpret facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence really support the claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to its audio, video, or multimedia version, noticing how each one shows the subject differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's opinion or purpose and explain how it is different from others' views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can examine how an author organizes a text and how each section helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and see how word choices affect meaning and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas affect each other in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop, and give a summary using evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use several pieces of evidence from a text to explain what it says and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 7.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare how a story or novel shows a time, place, or character with how it is described in real history, to see how fiction changes or uses history.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (e.g., lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to its audio, movie, stage, or multimedia version, noticing how each one uses different techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of a text, including how a drama or poem's form or structure contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the way a story, play, or poem is organized helps show its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (e.g., alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including figurative language, and explain how word choices affect the tone and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story or play, like setting and characters, work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea of a story and explain how it develops, giving a summary with evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use several pieces of evidence from a story or play to explain what it says and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use new words that are important for school subjects and know how to find out what unfamiliar words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference in feelings or ideas between words that have similar meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related to each other to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., personification) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like personification in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words are connected, and the slight differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check if their guess about a word's meaning is correct.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find how to say a word or what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from sentences or paragraphs to figure out the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin word parts to help understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or explain the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistent style and tone.
description_plain: Your child keeps a consistent style and tone in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of sentences to make their writing or speaking more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct spelling.
description_plain: Use correct spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off non-restrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, and dashes correctly to set off extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from Standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child notices when language is different from standard English and learns ways to improve their writing and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns in writing and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child can find and correct mistakes when pronouns don't match in number or person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses intensive pronouns like myself and ourselves correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, and possessive).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns in the correct form, like subject, object, or possessive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, using formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, and sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, music, or other media to presentations to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas clearly in order, using facts and details, while speaking with good eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument and tell which claims are supported by evidence and which are not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, and orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand information from different sources like videos or charts and explain how it relates to a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed, draw conclusions, and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can summarize main ideas, make conclusions, and show understanding of different viewpoints by reflecting and restating.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions with detailed comments that add to the discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow discussion rules, set goals and deadlines, and take on roles when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses evidence to think about and discuss ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group discussions, listen to others, build on their ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, both for longer projects and for shorter assignments, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literary nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child can use reading skills to understand and learn from nonfiction stories and articles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literature.
description_plain: Your child can use reading skills to understand and learn from stories and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find proof from stories or information texts to support their thoughts and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different sources, check if those sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects using several sources and adjust their questions as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the internet, to type and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to complete a writing task in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the internet to write, publish, and work with others, typing well enough to finish a writing task in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With some help, your child can plan, revise, edit, and improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense for the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words, details, and sensory language to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop the story or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing the main character or narrator, and organizing events in a way that makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using good writing techniques, clear details, and a logical order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that fits with the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words, including subject-related vocabulary, to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect ideas and show how they relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by adding facts, definitions, examples, quotes, and other details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, organize their ideas clearly, and use things like headings, pictures, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that clearly explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that matches and wraps up their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can back up their opinions with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child can state their main point and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend informational texts and nonfiction in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 6.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction texts on grade-appropriate topics independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another author.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two authors tell about the same events differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments in a text, telling which claims are supported by reasons and evidence and which are not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from words, pictures, and data to understand a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out an author's point of view or purpose and explain how the text shows it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a text fit together and help develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or descriptive meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and developed in a text (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a key person, event, or idea is introduced and developed in a text using examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgements.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from a text to explain what it says and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 6.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems on grade-appropriate topics independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories and poems or different types of books to see how they handle similar themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story, play, or poem to listening to or watching it, noticing the differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a specific sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the whole story and helps build the main idea, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including figurative language, and explain how word choice affects tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story or play's plot develops and how characters change as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme of a story and explain how it is shown through details, and can summarize the story without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from a story or poem to explain what it says and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use grade-level vocabulary and look up new words when needed to understand or express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., bullheaded, willful, firm, persistent, resolute).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings or associations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related to each other to better understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g. verbal irony, puns) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like irony and puns in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check that they have figured out the correct meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both print and online, to find word meanings, pronunciations, or parts of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from sentences or paragraphs to figure out the meaning of unknown words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, and secede).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to help understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of unknown words or phrases using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening. Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects (e.g., emphasizing the actor or the action; expressing uncertainty or describing a state contrary to fact).
description_plain: Your child can use correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening, including using different verb forms to create effects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct spelling.
description_plain: Use correct spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child can use an ellipsis (...) to show that something has been left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation like commas, ellipses, and dashes to show pauses or breaks in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verbals, voice, and mood.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and fix mistakes when verb forms, voice, or mood change incorrectly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive mood.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in different moods (like statements, commands, questions, possibilities, or wishes) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in both active and passive voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what verbals (like gerunds, participles, and infinitives) do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other visuals in presentations to make information clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share their ideas and findings using strong reasons and details, while speaking loudly enough and making eye contact.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a speaker is arguing, judge if their reasons and evidence make sense, and notice when something doesn't fit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can understand why information is shared in different ways and think about the reasons behind it, like social or political goals.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views based on the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child listens to new ideas from others and changes or explains their own opinions when the evidence supports it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that connect what different people are saying and answer others with good evidence or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow group discussion rules, keep track of goals and deadlines, and help decide who does what in a group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses what they learned to ask questions and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, build on others' ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes over several days for big projects and sometimes in shorter sessions, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literary nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child uses 8th-grade reading skills to understand and learn from nonfiction writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literature.
description_plain: Your child uses 8th-grade reading skills to understand and learn from stories and literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child finds evidence in stories or informational texts to support their thoughts, reflections, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying, giving proper credit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new, related questions to explore the topic further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses technology like the internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child plans, writes, revises, and edits their writing to make it clearer and better for their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that makes sense and reflects on the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and vivid details to describe actions and experiences clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another, and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to show the order of events, changes in time or place, and how events are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection to develop the story, events, or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, and tells events in a clear, natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and a well-organized sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits and supports the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style appropriate for the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with well-chosen, relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child supports their topic with facts, definitions, examples, and quotes that fit well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into categories, and use helpful features like headings, graphics, or multimedia.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic clearly by choosing, organizing, and analyzing relevant information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style that fits the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counter-claims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect their claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can support their argument with logical reasons and evidence from reliable sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main argument, explain how it is different from other viewpoints, and organize reasons and evidence logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their ideas with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend informational texts and nonfiction in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 8.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand nonfiction texts that are appropriate for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts on the same topic, find where they disagree, and explain the differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence make sense and noticing when evidence doesn't fit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e.g., print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child can think about the good and bad points of using different ways like print, video, or digital to share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view, perspective and purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's opinion and purpose and see how the author deals with different or opposing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can closely examine a paragraph to see how each sentence helps explain or develop a main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and feeling of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies, or categories).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text connects or compares people, ideas, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a text, explain how it develops with supporting details, and give a summary using evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the strongest evidence from a text to explain what it says and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas and poetry, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 8.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories or religious works, including describing how the material is rendered new.
description_plain: Your child can explain how modern stories use ideas, events, or characters from old myths, traditional tales, or religious stories, and describe what makes them new or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.
description_plain: Your child can compare a movie or play version of a story to the original text and explain how the director's or actors' choices change the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the audience or reader (e.g., created through the use of dramatic irony) create such effects as suspense or humor.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different points of view in stories create feelings like suspense or humor.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more stories are organized and explain how those differences affect the meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in stories, including figurative language, and explain how word choices affect the story's mood and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a story or drama propel the action, reveal aspects of a character, or provoke a decision.
description_plain: Your child can explain how specific dialogue or events in a story move the plot forward, show character traits, or cause decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to the characters, setting, and plot; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea in a story, explain how it develops with the characters, setting, and plot, and give a summary with evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the best evidence from stories to explain what they say and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can correctly use a wide range of words for school and specific subjects, and can figure out new words on their own when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can correctly use a wide range of words for school and specific subjects, and can figure out new words on their own when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the slight differences in meaning between words that are similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the slight differences in meaning between words that are similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like exaggerations or contradictions in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like exaggerations or contradictions in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check if they have figured out the correct meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check if they have figured out the correct meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find how to say a word, what it means, its part of speech, history, or correct use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find how to say a word, what it means, its part of speech, history, or correct use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the surrounding words and sentences to figure out the meaning of unknown words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the surrounding words and sentences to figure out the meaning of unknown words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g. conceive, conception, conceivable).
description_plain: Your child knows how words change form and meaning, like from 'conceive' to 'conception,' and uses them correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g. conceive, conception, conceivable).
description_plain: Your child knows how words change form and meaning, like from 'conceive' to 'conception,' and uses them correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11–12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of difficult or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11–12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of difficult or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.
description_plain: Your child can change the way sentences are put together for effect and can use resources to help understand or write more complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.
description_plain: Your child can change the way sentences are put together for effect and can use resources to help understand or write more complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and sentence styles to make their meaning clear when reading, writing, or speaking.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and sentence styles to make their meaning clear when reading, writing, or speaking.

description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources and types of media to make smart decisions and suggest solutions, checking if the sources are trustworthy.
