subject: Language Arts
description: Represent phonemes in simple words, using letter-sound relationships.
description_plain: Your child can write simple words by matching sounds to letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills when encoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use letter sounds to spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write the letters used to represent vowel phonemes and those used to represent consonants, knowing that every syllable has a vowel.
description_plain: Your child can write letters for vowel and consonant sounds and understands every syllable has a vowel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment all the phonemes in two and three-phoneme syllables and represent those phonemes with letters.
description_plain: Your child can break two- and three-sound words into individual sounds and write letters for each sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply sound-letter concepts.
description_plain: Your child understands how sounds match letters and uses this when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write left to right using appropriate spacing between words.
description_plain: Your child writes from left to right and leaves spaces between words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write upper and lower manuscript letters, with reference to a model.
description_plain: Your child can write uppercase and lowercase letters by looking at a model.

subject: Language Arts
description: Match upper and lower case manuscript letters.
description_plain: Your child can match uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply handwriting skills.
description_plain: Your child practices and uses handwriting skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 3)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 3)

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 4)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 4)

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of books by a favorite author and express opinions about them).
description_plain: With help, your child takes part in group research and writing projects, like reading books by the same author and sharing opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child uses digital tools to create and share writing, sometimes working with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child listens to friends’ ideas and adds details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above).
description_plain: With help, your child writes pieces that fit the topic and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide a reaction to what happened.
description_plain: With help, your child tells about one or more events by drawing, speaking, and writing in the order they happened and shares how they feel about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: With help, your child writes or draws to explain a topic by naming it and giving some facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book (e.g., My favorite book is…).
description_plain: With help, your child writes or draws to share their opinion about a topic or book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent-reader texts with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads simple books with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look almost the same by noticing which letters and sounds are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read 50 common high-frequency words by sight from a research-based word list.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read 50 common words by sight.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled closed-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can sound out simple words with short vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondence by producing the primary or most frequent sound(s) for each consonant and the five major vowels.
description_plain: Your child knows the main sounds for each consonant and the five vowels.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses letter sounds to figure out new words when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add, substitute, and delete individual phonemes in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change sounds in simple words to make new words by adding, changing, or taking away sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel (long and short vowels), and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme words. (This does not include CVCs (Consonant-Vowel-Consonant) ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle, and last sounds in three-sound words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words. Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words. Blend spoken phonemes to form one-syllable words (e.g., /m/ /a/ /n/).
description_plain: Your child can count and say syllables in words and blend or break apart sounds to make or say words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can find and say words that rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and produce sounds (phonemes) in a spoken word.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the individual sounds in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands that words have parts like syllables and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can name all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child knows that words in print are separated by spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the difference between a letter and a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between a letter and a whole word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify that a sentence is made up of a group of words.
description_plain: Your child knows that a sentence is made up of several words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child knows that spoken words are written down using letters in a certain order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works, like how to follow words on a page and what letters and words look like.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: With help, your child joins group reading activities and understands what is read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: With help, your child can find things that are the same and different in two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can find the reasons an author gives to explain their points in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in which they appear (e.g., what person, place, thing, or idea in the text an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how the pictures and words in a book go together.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, name the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can name the author and illustrator of a book and explain what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of informational text; identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can recognize types of informational books and point out the front cover, back cover, and title page.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don't know in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main idea of a book and remember important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: With help, your child joins group reading activities and understands the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to kindergarten literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how the pictures in a story relate to what is happening in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, name the author and illustrator of a story and define the role of each in telling the story.
description_plain: With help, your child can name the author and illustrator of a story and explain what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts (e.g., storybooks, poems); identify the front cover, back cover, and title of a book.
description_plain: Your child can recognize types of stories like books and poems and point out the front cover, back cover, and title.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don't know in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell who is in a story, where it happens, and what big events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.
description_plain: With help, your child can retell familiar stories and include important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and accurately use grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (e.g., quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (e.g., wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that are important for school subjects and describe actions, feelings, or topics clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their synonyms and antonyms.
description_plain: Your child understands words better by knowing their similar and opposite meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain the meanings of common sayings and expressions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors (e.g., as pretty as a picture) in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain simple comparisons like similes and metaphors in stories or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauri), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use dictionaries and other tools to find how to say words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues in sentences, like examples or explanations, to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., telegraph, photograph, autograph).
description_plain: Your child uses common word parts from Greek and Latin to help understand the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion).
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like in presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like in small groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose punctuation marks to create a certain effect in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that clearly show their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly for their grade level and use references to check if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma before words like 'and,' 'but,' or 'or' when joining two sentences together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing what someone says or quoting from a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.
description_plain: Use correct capitalization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and organize one or more paragraphs that contain: a topic sentence, supporting details, and a conclusion that is appropriate to the writing task. (Construction of paragraph(s) should demonstrate command of Writing standards 1-3.)
description_plain: Your child writes paragraphs with a clear main idea, supporting details, and a conclusion that fits the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their).
description_plain: Your child uses commonly confused words correctly, like to/too/two and there/their.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child can write complete sentences and fix sentences that are incomplete or run on too long.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use phrases that show location, time, or direction, like 'under the table' or 'after lunch.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (e.g., a small red bag rather than a red small bag).
description_plain: Your child puts adjectives in the right order in sentences, like saying 'a small red bag' instead of 'a red small bag.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries (e.g., can, may, must) to convey various conditions.
description_plain: Your child uses helping words like can, may, and must to show different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive verb tenses (e.g., I was walking; I am walking; I will be walking).
description_plain: Your child forms and uses verb tenses that show ongoing actions, like 'I am walking' or 'I was walking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns (who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (where, when, why).
description_plain: Your child uses words like who, which, where, and when to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 4 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations).
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like in presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like in small group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add sound recordings or pictures to their presentations to help explain their main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can clearly and in order talk about a topic, story, or experience, using facts and details to support their main points and speaking at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can pick out the reasons and evidence a speaker gives to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate parts of something they heard or saw in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding based on the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main ideas from a discussion and share their own thoughts about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions to better understand the topic, and add comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow the group’s rules during discussions and do their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses what they learned to talk about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Transcribe ideas legibly and fluently with appropriate spacing and indentation.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly and spaces words and paragraphs correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and write cursive letters, upper and lower case.
description_plain: Your child can read and write cursive letters, both uppercase and lowercase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply handwriting skills.
description_plain: Your child shows good handwriting skills and uses them when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write often, sometimes working on longer projects over several days and sometimes writing shorter pieces in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to understand informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to literature.
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to understand stories and literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction books to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes, categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find important information, take notes, organize it, and list where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to complete a writing task.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses technology like the internet to write, share, and work with others, and types well enough to finish writing tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, writes, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense for the story or experience they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use specific words and details that help readers picture what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'next,' 'then,' or 'finally' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use conversations and descriptions to show what characters do and how they feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can set up a story by introducing the situation and characters and organizing the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that fits the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and vocabulary related to the topic to explain it clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas in writing using words like 'another,' 'for example,' and 'because.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by adding facts, definitions, examples, or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, group related information together, and use things like headings, pictures, or media to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic and share ideas and information clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that matches the opinion they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child links their opinions and reasons using words like 'for instance' and 'in order to.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons for their opinion and support them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, share their opinion, and organize related ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic and give reasons and facts to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in the sentence to help figure out or correct words they don't know, and will reread if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with understanding and for a clear reason.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and correctly so they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read grade level words accurately.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of word parts like roots and endings to read grade-level words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of the six syllable patterns to read grade level words accurately.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of syllable patterns to read grade-level words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences to read unfamiliar multisyllabic words accurately.
description_plain: Your child uses all letter sounds to read new multi-syllable words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills in decoding multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use phonics and word parts to read longer words both in and out of reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 4.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level informational texts on subjects like history, science, and technology on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from two texts about the same topic to write or talk knowledgeably about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support their points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in pictures, charts, graphs, or timelines and how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus, and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand account (someone who was there) and a secondhand account (someone who heard about it) of the same event, and describe how they are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, and problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by time order, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words or phrases in a text about a fourth grade topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain events, steps, ideas, or concepts in nonfiction texts, including what happened and why, using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 4.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare how similar themes and story events are shown in stories and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect what happens in a story or play to a picture or spoken version, and point out where each matches the original text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first-and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different stories are told, including the difference between stories told by a character (first person) and stories told by a narrator (third person).

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the overall structure and major differences between poetry, drama, and prose.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poetry, plays, and regular stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words, phrases, and figurative language found in stories, poetry, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures, including those that allude to significant characters.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in stories and poems from different cultures, including special references to important characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail, using specific information from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem and summarize what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a story to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships (e.g., however, although, nevertheless, similarly, moreover, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can correctly use grade-level words, including words that show contrast, addition, or other relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses the relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain the meaning of common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what similes and metaphors mean in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say words and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the words and ideas around it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., photograph, photosynthesis).
description_plain: Your child understands common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (e.g., dialects, registers) used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain differences in how English is spoken or written in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can change sentences by expanding, combining, or shortening them to make writing clearer or more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words correctly and use references to check spelling if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child can use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to show the titles of books and other works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no (e.g., Yes, thank you), to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence (e.g., It's true, isn't it?), and to indicate direct address (e.g., Is that you, Steve?).
description_plain: Your child knows how to use commas with words like yes and no, in questions added to sentences, and when talking directly to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma after the first part of a sentence when it introduces what comes next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and organize one or more paragraphs that contain: a topic sentence, supporting details, and a conclusion that is appropriate to the writing task.
description_plain: Your child can write paragraphs that start with a main idea, include supporting details, and end with a fitting conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions (e.g., either/or, neither/nor).
description_plain: Your child uses pairs of connecting words like either/or and neither/nor correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when the verb tense changes incorrectly in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states, and conditions.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use different verb tenses to show when things happen or their order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect (e.g., I had walked; I have walked; I will have walked) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verb tenses that show actions completed in the past, present, or future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections do in sentences and point out how they work in examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, sound) and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, sounds, or other media to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can give a clear report or opinion, organize their ideas in order, use facts and details, and speak at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can summarize what a speaker says and explain how their points are supported by reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information they hear or see in different ways, like from a book read aloud, a video, or a chart.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions based on information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child can summarize important ideas from discussions and make conclusions based on what was learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions in a discussion and add helpful comments to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow the rules for group discussions and do their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading or studying the topic and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, build on others' ideas, and share their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Transcribe ideas legibly and fluently with appropriate spacing and indentation.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly and smoothly with correct spacing and paragraph indentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply handwriting skills.
description_plain: Your child shows good handwriting skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and write cursive letters, upper and lower case.
description_plain: Your child can read and write cursive letters, both uppercase and lowercase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write often, sometimes working on longer projects over several days and sometimes writing shorter pieces in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses 5th grade reading skills to understand informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to literature.
description_plain: Your child uses 5th grade reading skills to understand stories and literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction texts to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find important information from their own experiences or from books and websites, summarize it, and list where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic and to answer a specific question.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills in order to complete a writing task.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses technology like the internet to write, publish, and work with others, and types well enough to finish writing tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, writes, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense for the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and details that appeal to the senses to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses different words and phrases to show the order of events in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue and description, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions to tell about events and show how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting up the situation, introducing the narrator and characters, and organizing the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and a logical sequence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., in contrast, especially).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas within and between topics using words like 'in contrast' or 'especially.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by adding facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, give an overview, group related information, and use things like headings, pictures, or media to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that clearly explain a topic and share ideas and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that matches the opinion they presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., consequently, specifically).
description_plain: Your child links their opinions and reasons using words like 'consequently' and 'specifically.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons for their opinion and support them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic or text, state their opinion, and organize their ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces about topics or texts, giving reasons and information to support their view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the surrounding words to check and correct how they read and understand words, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with a clear reason and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and correctly so they can understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots to accurately read unfamiliar words.
description_plain: Your child knows common Greek and Latin word parts and uses them to read new words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of morphology to read grade level words accurately.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge about word parts to read grade-level words accurately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of the six syllable patterns to read grade level words accurately.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of six syllable types to read grade-level words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences to accurately read unfamiliar multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Your child uses all letter-sound rules to read unfamiliar multisyllable words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills in decoding multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word study skills to read longer words both in and out of reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend informational text, including history/social studies, science and technological texts, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 5.
description_plain: By the end of 5th grade, your child can read and understand informational texts about history, science, and technology on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different books or articles on the same topic to write or talk knowledgeably about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support their points, and can identify which reasons support which points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different books or websites to quickly find answers or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can compare different accounts of the same event or topic and notice important similarities and differences in how they are told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, and problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts organize events or ideas, like by time order or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of general and subject-specific words and phrases in a fifth-grade text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, ideas, or concepts are connected in a nonfiction text, using specific information from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can accurately quote from a text to explain what it says and to support their own ideas or conclusions.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 5.
description_plain: By the end of 5th grade, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (e.g., mysteries and adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories in the same type, like mysteries or adventures, and explain how they handle similar themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the purpose, meaning, or tone of the text (e.g., graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, and poem).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or videos help show the meaning or feeling of a story, poem, or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the narrator's or speaker's point of view affects how the story is told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or stanzas fit together to form the whole story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including metaphors and similes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast characters, settings, or events in a story or play, using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details of the text; include how characters in story or drama respond to challenges, how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic, and a summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message of a story, play, or poem by looking at details and how characters or speakers respond to challenges.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can accurately quote from a story or poem to explain what it says and to support their own ideas.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child notices patterns when solving problems and uses these patterns to help with reasoning.

description_plain: Your child looks for and uses the structure or organization in math problems to solve them more easily.

description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child understands how to think about math problems both in general ideas and with actual numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand math problems and keep working on them until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Use the Multiplication Rule for independent events to understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A and B occurring together is the product of their probabilities, and use this characterization to determine if they are independent.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication to find the chance of two independent events happening together and decide if the events are independent.

subject: Math
description: Use the Multiplication Rule for independent events to understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A and B occurring together is the product of their probabilities, and use this characterization to determine if they are independent.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication to find the chance of two independent events happening together and decide if the events are independent.

subject: Math
description: Use the Multiplication Rule for independent events to understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A and B occurring together is the product of their probabilities, and use this characterization to determine if they are independent.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication to find the chance of two independent events happening together and decide if the events are independent.

subject: Math
description: Use the Multiplication Rule for independent events to understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A and B occurring together is the product of their probabilities, and use this characterization to determine if they are independent.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication to find the chance of two independent events happening together and decide if the events are independent.

subject: Math
description: Describe events as subsets of a sample space using characteristics of the outcomes, or as unions, intersections, or complements of other events.
description_plain: Your child can describe events by grouping outcomes and use combinations like unions, intersections, or opposites to explain them.

description_plain: Your child can describe events by grouping outcomes and use combinations like unions, intersections, or opposites to explain them.

description: Describe events as subsets of a sample space using characteristics of the outcomes, or as unions, intersections, or complements of other events.
description_plain: Your child can describe events by grouping outcomes and use combinations like unions, intersections, or opposites to explain them.

subject: Math
description: Understand independence and conditional probability and use them to interpret data.
description_plain: Your child understands what it means for events to be independent or connected and uses this to understand data.

Key features include: intercepts; intervals where the function is increasing, decreasing, positive, or negative; relative maximums and minimums. Focus on linear, absolute value, quadratic, exponential and piecewise-defined functions (limited to the aforementioned functions).
description_plain: Your child can explain important parts of graphs and tables of functions, like where they go up or down, cross axes, or have maximum or minimum points, and draw graphs from descriptions.

description: Understand that a function from one set (called the domain) to another set (called the range) assigns to each element of the domain exactly one element of the range. If f is a function and x is an element of its domain, then f(x) denotes the output of f corresponding to the input x. The graph of f is the graph of the equation y = f(x).
description_plain: Your child understands that a function pairs each input with exactly one output and can read and use function notation and graphs.

description: Identify the effect on the graph of replacing f(x) by f(x) + k, kf(x), f(kx), and f(x+k) for specific values of k (both positive and negative); find the value of k given the graphs. Experiment with cases and illustrate an explanation of the effects on the graph using technology. Include recognizing even and odd functions from their graphs and algebraic expressions for them. Extend from linear, quadratic and exponential functions to include polynomial, radical, logarithmic, rational, sine, cosine, and exponential functions, and piecewise-defined functions.
description_plain: Your child can see how changing a function by adding, multiplying, or shifting affects its graph and can use technology to explore these changes with many types of functions.

description: Know and apply the Remainder and Factor Theorem: For a polynomial p(x) and a number a, the remainder on division by (x – a) is p(a), so p(a) = 0 if and only if (x – a) is a factor of p(x).

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., After dinner that night, we went looking for them).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words for conversations, school subjects, and special topics, including words that show where or when something happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, and wondered).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that are similar but show different feelings or levels of certainty.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their uses (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life examples, like describing friendly or helpful people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and non-literal meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the literal and figurative meanings of words and phrases in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can notice small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or glossaries, in print or online, to find the exact meaning of important words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child can use a familiar root word to help understand a new word with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of a new word by adding a known prefix or suffix to a familiar word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can find or clear up the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written Standard English.
description_plain: Your child can notice and follow the differences between spoken and written Standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that make their writing or speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.
description_plain: Form and use possessives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.
description_plain: Use commas in addresses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write one or more paragraphs that explain a main idea within a topic and support it with details and conclusions/closure.
description_plain: Your child can write paragraphs that explain a main idea with supporting details and a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'and,' 'but,' 'because,' and 'although' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'bigger,' 'biggest,' 'more quickly,' and 'most quickly' correctly when comparing things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child can make sure the subject and verb in a sentence match, and that pronouns refer to the right nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple verb tenses (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk).
description_plain: Your child can use simple verb tenses like past, present, and future correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use both regular verbs (like 'walked') and irregular verbs (like 'ran') correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).
description_plain: Your child can use abstract nouns like 'childhood' in writing and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make regular and irregular nouns plural, like 'cats' and 'children.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child will speak in complete sentences when needed to give clear answers or more information.

subject: Language Arts
