description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child can record stories or poems reading smoothly and clearly, and add pictures or visuals to highlight important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can tell about a topic, story, or experience with facts and details, speaking clearly at a good pace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what a speaker says, giving good explanations and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main ideas and important details from something read aloud or shown in different ways, like videos or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding based on the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain their own ideas and what they understand during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to make sure they understand, stay on topic, and connect their comments to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for discussions, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses what they know to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, listen to others, build on their ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pattern-based words.
description_plain: Pattern-based words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Irregular words.
description_plain: Irregular words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-level appropriate words in English, as found in a research-based list, including:
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-appropriate words correctly using a research-based list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include common, transparent prefixes and suffixes (e.g., re-, pre-, sub-, un-, dis-, mis-; -able, -ness, -ful, -tion).
description_plain: Your child can spell words by adding common beginnings and endings like re-, un-, -ful, and -ness.

subject: Language Arts
description: Combine all basic syllable types: closed, VCe (Vowel-Consonant-silent e), open, vowel team, vowel-r, and consonant le.
description_plain: Your child can read and spell words using different syllable types like closed syllables, silent e, vowel teams, and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell regular two-and three-syllable words that:
description_plain: Your child can spell regular two- and three-syllable words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell singular and plural possessives (e.g., teacher's, teachers').
description_plain: Your child can spell words that show ownership, like teacher's or teachers'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify language of origin for words, as noted in dictionaries.
description_plain: Your child can recognize where words come from by using dictionary information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell single-syllable words with less common and complex graphemes (e.g., ough, augh, old, -ind, -ost, -ild families).
description_plain: Your child can spell single-syllable words with tricky letter patterns like ough or augh.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply spelling conventions and patterns.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses common spelling rules and patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Standard ends at grade 2.
description_plain: Standard ends at grade 2.

subject: Language Arts
description: Transcribe ideas legibly in cursive and manuscript, with appropriate spacing and indentation.
description_plain: Your child can write neatly in both cursive and print, using proper spacing and indentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and write cursive letters, upper and lower case.
description_plain: Your child can read and write uppercase and lowercase cursive letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply handwriting skills.
description_plain: Your child can show and use good handwriting skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods, choosing topics and styles for fun, school, or other purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 4)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 4)

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from their own experiences or find information from books and websites, take short notes, and sort facts into groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child can use technology to write, publish, and work with others online.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can plan, write, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With adult support, your child can write pieces that are organized and fit the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Provide a sense of closure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'next,' and 'finally' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue and describe what characters do, think, and feel to show what happens in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can set up a story by introducing the situation and characters, and organize the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can end their informative writing with a closing statement or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child can use words like also, and, or but to connect ideas in the same group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by giving facts, definitions, and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, group related facts, and add pictures to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or give information about a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can end their opinion writing with a closing statement or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words like because and for example to connect opinions with reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, state an opinion, and list reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, using reasons to support one's point of view.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that give reasons to support their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or double-check the meaning and pronunciation of words, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with expression, getting better each time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and a clear reason for reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-appropriate words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of the six syllable types to read grade-level words accurately.
description_plain: Your child can use knowledge of syllable types to read words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize common beginnings and endings of words and knows what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills in decoding one-syllable or multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word parts to read one- and multi-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 3.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level informational texts on history, science, and technical topics on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences in the main points and important details of two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs connect, like showing cause and effect or order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur).
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in a text to understand when, where, why, and how important events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish one's own point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the author’s point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use text features like keywords and sidebars to quickly find information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words and phrases in a text about a third-grade topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps in a process are related by time, order, or cause and effect in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount and paraphrase the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, retell important details, and explain how they support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what they read, using the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 3.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child can compare the themes, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how the pictures in a story help show what is happening or how the characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish one's own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own point of view and that of the story’s narrator or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about different parts of stories, plays, and poems, using words like chapter, scene, or stanza, and explain how each part builds on what came before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from non-literal language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when the author uses nonliteral language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can describe story characters and explain how their actions move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount and paraphrase stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories from different cultures and explain the main message or lesson using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what they read in stories, using the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe (e.g., When other kids are happy that makes me happy).
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they have learned, including describing things with adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify synonyms and antonyms to distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child can find words that mean the same or the opposite to understand small differences in meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life examples, like describing foods as spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can tell the small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use glossaries and simple dictionaries, in books or online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from sentences to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child can use the meanings of smaller words to guess what compound words mean, like birdhouse or notebook.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child can use a word they know to help figure out the meaning of a new word that has the same base (like addition and additional).

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
description_plain: Your child can understand new words by adding prefixes to words they already know (like happy and unhappy).

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned to help spell new words (like cage and badge).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child uses apostrophes correctly to make contractions and show when something belongs to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child uses commas in the greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the names of holidays, products, and places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With assistance, link sentences into a simple, cohesive paragraph that contains: a main idea, supporting details, and a conclusion.
description_plain: With help, your child can write a simple paragraph with a main idea, details, and a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and use declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use different types of sentences like statements, questions, commands, and exclamations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences using frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, yet, so).
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer or change their order using words like and, but, or, yet, and so.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use interjections (e.g., Yes! That is mine; Yes, that is mine!)
description_plain: Your child can use words that show strong feelings, like 'Yes!' or 'Wow!'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses adjectives and adverbs correctly to describe things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past, present, and future tenses of frequently occurring regular and irregular verbs (e.g. sat, hit, and told).
description_plain: Your child can use past, present, and future verb forms correctly, including tricky verbs like sat, hit, and told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child can use reflexive pronouns like myself and ourselves correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).
description_plain: Your child can use irregular plural nouns like feet, children, and mice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group).
description_plain: Your child can use words that name groups of things, like 'group'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences and add details or explanations when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can record themselves reading stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or talk about something that happened, using clear sentences and important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone else says to better understand or learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe the main ideas from something they heard or saw.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more information or for things to be explained if they don't understand during a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations by connecting their ideas to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can join group conversations about grade 2 topics with classmates and adults, sharing and building on ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pattern-based words (e.g., which, kind, have).
description_plain: Your child can spell words that follow common spelling patterns, like which and kind.

subject: Language Arts
description: Irregular words (e.g., against, many, enough, does).
description_plain: Your child can spell irregular words that don’t follow usual rules, like against and many.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-level appropriate words in English, as found in a research-based list, including:
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-appropriate words from a researched list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Changing y to i (e.g., cried, babies).
description_plain: Your child can spell words that change y to i when adding endings, like cried and babies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Dropping silent e (e.g., smiled, paving).
description_plain: Your child can spell words that drop the silent e before adding endings, like smiled and paving.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consonant doubling (e.g., running, slipped).
description_plain: Your child can spell words that double consonants before adding endings, like running and slipped.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, spell words with suffixes that require:
description_plain: With help, your child can spell words with endings that follow these special rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes (e.g., un-, re-, en-, -ful, -ment, -less).
description_plain: Your child can spell words with common beginnings and endings like un-, re-, -ful, and -less.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include familiar compound words (e.g., houseboat, yellowtail).
description_plain: Your child can spell familiar compound words made by joining two words, like houseboat.

subject: Language Arts
description: Combine closed, open, vowel teams, vowel-r, and CVe (Consonant-Vowel-silent e) syllables (e.g., compete, robot, violet, understand).
description_plain: Your child can spell two- and three-syllable words with different syllable types, like compete and robot.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, spell two- and three-syllable words that:
description_plain: With help, your child can spell longer words with two or three syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plurals and possessives (e.g., its, it's).
description_plain: Your child can spell plurals and possessive words correctly, like its and it's.

subject: Language Arts
description: Homophones (e.g., bear, bare; past, passed).
description_plain: Your child can spell words that sound the same but have different meanings, like bear and bare.

subject: Language Arts
description: Contractions (e.g., we'll, I'm, they've, don't).
description_plain: Your child can spell contractions like we'll, I'm, and don't.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vowel-r combinations (e.g., turn, star, third, four, for).
description_plain: Your child can spell words with vowel-r sounds, like turn and star.

subject: Language Arts
description: Less common vowel teams for long vowels (e.g., ow, oo, au, ou, ue).
description_plain: Your child can spell words with less common vowel teams like ow, oo, and au.

subject: Language Arts
description: Complex consonant blends (e.g., scr, str, squ).
description_plain: Your child can spell words with blends of consonants like scr, str, and squ.

subject: Language Arts
description: Position-based patterns (e.g., ch, -tch; k, -ck; -ge, -dge).
description_plain: Your child can spell words with common letter patterns based on where sounds happen, like ch and -tch.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell on-level, regular, single-syllable words that include:
description_plain: Your child can spell regular one-syllable words using common patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills when encoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use sounds and word parts to spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vowels: /o/ = o, o_e, oa, ow (long o) /a/ = a, a_e, ai, ay, eigh (long a) Spelling
description_plain: Your child can spell long o and long a vowel sounds using different letter combinations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consonants: /s/ = s, ss, ce, ci, cy /f/ = f, ff, ph /k/ = c, k, ck
description_plain: Your child can spell certain consonant sounds using different letters, like /s/ with s, ss, or ce.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write the most common graphemes (letters or letter groups) for each phoneme. For example:
description_plain: Your child can write the most common letters or letter groups for each sound they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply sound-letter concepts.
description_plain: Your child can connect sounds to letters when writing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Transcribe ideas in manuscript with automaticity and proper spacing.
description_plain: Your child can write ideas neatly by hand with correct spacing between words without needing to think hard about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly in manuscript using correct letter formation.
description_plain: Your child will write clearly by forming letters correctly when writing by hand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply handwriting skills.
description_plain: Your child understands and practices good handwriting skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 3)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 3)

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 4)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 4)

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember things they have learned or find information to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations).
description_plain: Your child can work with others to research a topic by reading books and writing reports or recording observations.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child can use computers or tablets to write and share their work, including working with classmates.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by making changes and fixing mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above).
description_plain: With adult help, your child can write pieces that are organized and fit the purpose of the assignment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events; include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings; use temporal words to signal event order and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that tell about an event with details about actions, thoughts, and feelings, using words that show the order of events and a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, give facts and explanations, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words (e.g., because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic or book, give reasons for their opinion, use words to connect ideas, and write a closing sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or correct words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read words that are spelled in unusual ways for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can spot and read words where the spelling and sounds don’t always match but are common.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can sound out words that have common beginnings or endings like prefixes and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and apply all six syllable types to decode appropriate grade-level text.
description_plain: Your child knows the six types of syllables and uses them to read grade-level words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read words with common pairs of vowels (like 'ea' or 'ai').

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding one-syllable or two-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word parts to read one- and two-syllable words at grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade two.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand informational texts about history, science, and technology on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences between two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the reasons in a text support what the author is saying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or diagrams help make a text clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and what they want to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use parts of a text like captions, bold words, and indexes to find important information quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases related to grade 2 topics when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: With some help, your child can explain how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain the main topic of a multi-paragraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main topic of a text and explain what each paragraph focuses on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, proficiently and independently read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in a text complexity range determined by qualitative and quantitative measures appropriate to grade 2.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems on their own at grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the characters and settings from two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare characters and settings from two or more versions of the same story by different authors or cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child uses both pictures and words in a book to help understand the characters, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child can notice when characters have different points of view and can use different voices when reading their words aloud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is put together, including how it starts and how it ends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases supply rhythm (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how words and phrases create rhythm and meaning in stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to big events and problems in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including fables and folktales from different cultures, and explain the main lesson or moral.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to understand key details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., because).
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they learn from talking, reading, and listening, including words that show simple relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify synonyms and antonyms and distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (e.g. look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (eg. large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings
description_plain: With help, your child can find words that mean the same or the opposite and notice small differences in meaning by defining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like noticing cozy places at home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
description_plain: Your child can explain words by their category and important features, like saying a duck is a bird that swims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (e.g., colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups like colors or clothing to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With adult help, your child understands how words relate to each other and the different meanings they can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence around a word to help figure out what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can recognize common root words and their different forms, like look, looks, looked, and looking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common beginnings or endings of words to help understand their meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: With help, your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 2)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 2)

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas in dates and to separate words in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use the correct punctuation at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for names and dates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write multiple sentences in an order that supports a main idea or story.
description_plain: Your child can write several sentences in a logical order that supports a main idea or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: In response to prompts, distinguish between and identify declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between and name statements, questions, commands, and exclamations when given examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write and make longer simple and compound sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., during, beyond, toward).
description_plain: Your child uses common words that show location or time, like during, beyond, and toward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners (e.g., articles, demonstratives).
description_plain: Your child uses words like a, the, this, and that to point out things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child uses common connecting words like and, but, or, so, and because.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child uses describing words to add details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child uses action words to talk about things that happened before, are happening now, or will happen later.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their; anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child uses words like I, me, my, they, them, their, anyone, and everything to replace nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops. We hop.).
description_plain: Your child uses singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences, like He hops and We hop.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses common names, specific names, and shows ownership with nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word rules when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences when it fits the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas, thoughts, or feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child can clearly describe people, places, things, and events with helpful details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone else says to better understand or learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in something read aloud or shown in other ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions if they are confused about what is being discussed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations by responding to what others say, going back and forth several times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules in group talks, like listening carefully and speaking one at a time about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child joins conversations with classmates and adults about first-grade topics in small or big groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell unfamiliar words phonetically, applying phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child spells new words by sounding them out and using spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pattern based words (e.g., he, him, for, in, by, like).
description_plain: Your child spells words that follow common patterns, like he, him, for, in, by, and like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Irregular words (e.g., said, what, are, they, was).
description_plain: Your child spells common irregular words like said, what, are, they, and was.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-level appropriate words in English as found in a research-based list, including:
description_plain: Your child spells grade-level words from a trusted list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Words with two closed syllables (e.g., rabbit, wagon).
description_plain: Your child spells two-syllable words with closed syllables, like rabbit and wagon, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Common compound words (e.g., hotdog, mailbox).
description_plain: Your child spells common compound words like hotdog and mailbox with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Words that end in -y or -ly (e.g., smelly, gladly).
description_plain: Your child spells words ending in -y or -ly, like smelly and gladly, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, spell on-level two-syllable words, including:
description_plain: With help, your child spells two-syllable words at their level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adjectives with -er, -est, and no change to the base word (e.g. slower, slowest).
description_plain: Your child spells describing words with endings like -er and -est, such as slower and slowest, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Nouns with -s, -es, and no change to the base word (e.g., rugs, kisses).
description_plain: Your child spells plural nouns with endings like -s and -es, such as rugs and kisses, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verbs with -ing, -ed, -s, and no change in the base word (e.g., snowed, playing, jumps).
description_plain: Your child spells action words with endings like -ing, -ed, and -s, such as snowed, playing, and jumps, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, spell on-level words with inflectional endings:
description_plain: With help, your child spells words with common endings that change their form.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vowel-r combinations, including er, ar, or (e.g., car, her, stir, for, burn).
description_plain: Your child spells words with vowel and r sounds, like car, her, stir, for, and burn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Long vowel patterns spelled correctly, including VCe (Vowel-Consonant-silent e) (e.g., came, like), common vowel teams (e.g., boat, play, wait, see, team, right), and open syllables (e.g., go, cry).
description_plain: Your child spells long vowel sounds correctly, including words like came, like, boat, play, wait, see, team, right, go, and cry.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initial and final consonant blends (e.g., must, slab, plump).
description_plain: Your child spells words with blends of consonants at the start or end, like must, slab, and plump.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consonant graphemes including qu, x, and -ck; digraphs (e.g., thin, shop, when, much, sing); and doubled letters (e.g., off, will, mess).
description_plain: Your child spells sounds with special letter groups like qu, x, -ck, digraphs like th and sh, and doubled letters like ff and ll.

subject: Language Arts
description: Short vowels and single consonants.
description_plain: Your child spells short vowel sounds and single consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell common, regular, single-syllable words using:
description_plain: Your child spells common, regular one-syllable words using basic sounds and letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills when encoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to help spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and understand that each syllable is organized around a vowel sound.
description_plain: Your child understands that each syllable has one vowel sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally segment the phonemes in any single-syllable, spoken word.
description_plain: Your child can say the separate sounds in any one-syllable word out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply sound-letter concepts when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of sounds and letters when writing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write with appropriate spacing between letters and words.
description_plain: Your child writes with spaces between letters and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write the common grapheme (letter or letter group) for each phoneme.
description_plain: Your child writes the correct letters or letter groups for each sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write upper and lower case manuscript alphabet from memory using correct letter formation.
description_plain: Your child writes uppercase and lowercase letters neatly from memory.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and apply handwriting skills.
description_plain: Your child practices good handwriting skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 3)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 3)

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 4)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 4)

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child remembers or finds information to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of "how-to" books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).
description_plain: With help, your child works with others to research and write about topics, like reading 'how-to' books and writing steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: Your child can use different digital tools with help from adults to write and share their work, including working together with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: Your child can focus on a topic, listen to friends' ideas, and add details to make their writing better with adult support.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above).
description_plain: Your child can write with help from adults so their ideas are clear and organized for the task they are doing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write a story about something that happened, put the events in order, use words like 'first' or 'then,' and finish the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, give some facts, and wrap up their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion, say what they think about a topic or book, give a reason, and end their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books out loud with correct words, speed, and expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common tricky words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have endings like -ed or -ing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and apply all six syllable types when decoding grade level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use six different ways syllables sound to help read grade-level words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how many syllables are in a word by knowing each syllable has a vowel sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read simple one-syllable words that follow regular spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read common letter pairs that make one sound, like 'ch' or 'sh.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills in decoding one-syllable or multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word parts to read both short and longer words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Manipulate phonemes (add, substitute, and delete individual phonemes) in words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change sounds in words by adding, swapping, or taking away sounds to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally generate a series of rhyming words using a variety of phonograms (e.g., -ed, -ake, -ant, ain) and consonant blends (e.g., /bl/, /st/, /tr/).
description_plain: Your child can say a list of rhyming words using different word endings and sound blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken one-syllable words of three to five phonemes into individual phonemes (e.g., /s/p/l/a/t/).
description_plain: Your child can break apart spoken one-syllable words into each individual sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the first, middle, and last sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say whole one-syllable words by putting together individual sounds, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in words with only one syllable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made of sounds and syllables when listening and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., capitalization of first word and ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child can recognize that sentences start with a capital letter and end with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized, like reading left to right and knowing what a word or sentence looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read informational texts, including functional texts, history/social studies, science, and technical texts, appropriately complex for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read simple informational texts about history, science, and how-to topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: Your child can find basic similarities and differences between two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons an author gives to support their ideas in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a book to talk about the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between information from pictures and information from words in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book like headings and tables of contents to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to figure out what words and phrases mean in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text using key details.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in a text are connected using details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell you the main idea of a text and share important details from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions such as who, what, where, why, and how about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, why, and how about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read stories, drama, and poetry of appropriate complexity for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read stories, plays, and poems that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for this grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a story to describe the characters, where it happens, or what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information, drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.
description_plain: Your child can explain the difference between storybooks and books that give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help you imagine with your senses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe the characters, where the story happens, and the main events using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their main idea, central message, or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories with important details and explain the main idea or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions such as who, what, where, why, when, and how about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, why, when, and how about key parts of a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain‐specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words well for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain‐specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words well for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can understand small differences in meaning between similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can understand small differences in meaning between similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., euphemism, oxymoron) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain and analyze figures of speech like euphemisms and oxymorons in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., euphemism, oxymoron) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain and analyze figures of speech like euphemisms and oxymorons in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can check and confirm the meaning of a word or phrase they first guessed.

subject: Language Arts
