subject: Science
description: Observe, ask questions, and explain the differences between the characteristics of living and non-living things.
description_plain: Your child can notice and talk about how living things are different from things that are not alive.

subject: Science
description: Observe, ask questions, and explain how specialized structures found on a variety of plants and animals (including humans) help them sense and respond to their environment.
description_plain: Your child can observe and explain how parts of plants and animals help them sense and react to what’s around them.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate, and communicate information about how organisms use different body parts for survival.
description_plain: Your child can find and share information about how animals and plants use their body parts to survive.

subject: Science
description: Observe and ask questions about patterns of the motion of the sun, moon, and stars in the sky.
description_plain: Your child can watch and ask questions about how the sun, moon, and stars move in the sky.

subject: Science
description: Observe, describe, ask questions, and predict seasonal weather patterns; and how those patterns impact plants and animals (including humans).
description_plain: Your child can observe and talk about seasonal weather patterns and how these affect plants, animals, and people.

subject: Science
description: Observe, record, and ask questions about temperature, precipitation, and other weather data to identify patterns or changes in local weather.
description_plain: Your child can watch and record weather like temperature and rain to notice patterns or changes.

subject: Science
description: Design and evaluate a tool that helps people extend their senses.
description_plain: Your child can design and test a tool that helps people use their senses better.

subject: Science
description: Investigate how senses can detect light, sound, and vibrations even when they come from far away; use the collected evidence to develop and support an explanation.
description_plain: Your child can explore how their senses notice light, sound, and vibrations even from far away and explain what they find.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions and explain how factors can cause species to go extinct.
description_plain: Your child will ask questions and explain why some animals or plants disappear forever.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe how animals and plants are classified into groups and subgroups according to their similarities.
description_plain: Your child can create a way to group animals and plants based on how they are alike.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate, and communicate information to support an evidence-based explanation that plants and animals produce offspring of the same kind, but offspring are generally not identical to each other or their parents.
description_plain: Your child will find and share information showing that plants and animals have babies like themselves, but the babies usually look different from each other and their parents.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation describing how organisms obtain resources from the environment including materials that are used again by other organisms.
description_plain: Your child can explain how living things get what they need from their surroundings and how some materials are used again by other living things.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models about how living things use resources to grow and survive; design and evaluate habitats for organisms using earth materials.
description_plain: Your child will make and use models to show how living things use resources to grow and survive and design homes for animals using earth materials.

subject: Science
description: Observe, describe, and predict life cycles of animals and plants.
description_plain: Your child can watch, describe, and guess what will happen during the life stages of animals and plants.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate, and communicate information about the properties of Earth materials and investigate how humans use natural resources in everyday life.
description_plain: Your child will find and share information about Earth materials and how people use natural resources every day.

subject: Science
description: Design and evaluate ways to increase or reduce heat from friction between two objects.
description_plain: Your child can design and test ways to make heat from rubbing two objects together more or less.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out investigations which demonstrate how equal forces can balance objects and how unequal forces can push, pull, or twist objects, making them change their speed, direction, or shape.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do tests to show how equal forces can balance things and how different forces can push, pull, or twist objects to change how they move or their shape.

subject: Science
description: Use models to provide evidence that vibrating matter creates sound and sound can make matter vibrate.
description_plain: Your child can use models to show that vibrating things make sound and sound can make things vibrate.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out investigations demonstrating the effect of placing objects made with different materials in the path of a beam of light and predict how objects with similar properties will affect the beam of light.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do tests to see how objects made of different materials affect a beam of light and guess how similar objects will change the light.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model representing how life on Earth depends on energy from the Sun and energy from other organisms.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to show how life on Earth depends on energy from the Sun and from other living things.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, analyze, and communicate evidence that organisms need a source of energy, air, water, and certain temperature conditions to survive.
description_plain: Your child will find, study, and share proof that living things need energy, air, water, and certain temperatures to live.

subject: Science
description: Observe and explain the Sun's position at different times during a twenty-four-hour period and changes in the apparent shape of the Moon from one night to another.
description_plain: Your child can watch and explain how the Sun moves during the day and how the Moon’s shape changes from night to night.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument from evidence regarding positive and negative changes in water and land systems that impact humans and the environment.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence to explain how changes in water and land can be good or bad for people and nature.

subject: Science
description: Analyze patterns in weather conditions of various regions of the world and design, test, and refine solutions to protect humans from severe weather conditions.
description_plain: Your child can study weather patterns in different places and create, test, and improve ways to keep people safe from bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to represent that water can exist in different states and is found in oceans, glaciers, lakes, rivers, ponds, and the atmosphere.
description_plain: Your child will make and use models to show that water can be ice, liquid, or vapor and is found in oceans, glaciers, lakes, rivers, ponds, and the air.

subject: Science
description: Observe and investigate how wind and water change the shape of the land resulting in a variety of landforms.
description_plain: Your child can watch and explore how wind and water change the land to make different landforms.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate and communicate information about ways heat energy can cause change in objects or materials.
description_plain: Your child will find and share information about how heat can cause changes in objects or materials.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out investigations to gather evidence to support an explanation on how heating or cooling can cause a phase change in matter.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do tests to show how heating or cooling can change matter from one form to another.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out an investigation to determine that matter has mass, takes up space, and is recognized by its observable properties; use the collected evidence to develop and support an explanation.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do an investigation to show that matter has weight, takes up space, and has properties you can see, then explain what they learned.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument from evidence that organisms are interdependent.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain how living things depend on each other.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use system models to describe the flow of energy from the Sun to and among living organisms.
description_plain: Your child will create and use models to show how energy moves from the Sun to living things and between them.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out investigations to demonstrate ways plants and animals react to stimuli.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do tests to show how plants and animals respond to things around them.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to explain that plants and animals (including humans) have internal and external structures that serve various functions that aid in growth, survival, behavior, and reproduction.
description_plain: Your child can make and use models to explain how parts inside and outside plants and animals help them grow, survive, behave, and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation describing how the Sun is the primary source of energy impacting Earth systems.
description_plain: Your child will explain how the Sun is the main source of energy that affects Earth’s systems.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to describe how light and sound waves transfer energy.
description_plain: Your child can create and use models to show how light and sound waves carry energy.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out an investigation to explore how sound waves affect objects at varying distances.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do an investigation to see how sound waves affect objects at different distances.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions and investigate the relationship between light, objects, and the human eye.
description_plain: Your child will ask questions and explore how light, objects, and the human eye work together.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret environmental data to demonstrate that species either adapt and survive, or go extinct over time.
description_plain: Your child can study environmental data to show how species change to survive or disappear over time.

subject: Science
description: Define problem(s) and design solution(s) to minimize the effects of natural hazards.
description_plain: Your child will identify problems caused by natural hazards and design ways to reduce their effects.

subject: Science
description: Construct and support an evidence-based argument about the availability of water and its impact on life.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain how the amount of water available affects life.

subject: Science
description: Collect, analyze, and interpret data to explain weather and climate patterns.
description_plain: Your child will collect and study data to explain patterns in weather and climate.

subject: Science
description: Develop and/or revise a model using various rock types, fossil location, and landforms to show evidence that Earth's surface has changed over time.
description_plain: Your child can make or change models using rocks, fossils, and landforms to show how Earth’s surface has changed over time.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out an investigation to explore and explain the interactions between Earth's major systems and the impact on Earth's surface materials and processes.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do an investigation to explore how Earth’s systems interact and affect the surface and materials.

subject: Science
description: Use models to explain seismic waves and their effect on the Earth.
description_plain: Your child can use models to explain what seismic waves are and how they affect the Earth.

subject: Science
description: Engage in argument from evidence on the use and impact of renewable and nonrenewable resources to generate electricity.
description_plain: Your child will discuss using evidence how renewable and nonrenewable resources are used to make electricity and their effects.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to demonstrate magnetic forces.
description_plain: Your child can create and use a model to show how magnetic forces work.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model that explains how energy is moved from place to place through electric currents.
description_plain: Your child will make and use a model to explain how energy moves through electric currents.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to demonstrate how a system transfers energy from one object to another even when the objects are not touching.
description_plain: Your child can create and use a simple model to show how energy moves from one object to another, even if they are not touching.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument based on evidence that inherited characteristics can be affected by behavior and/or environmental conditions.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how inherited traits can change because of behavior or the environment.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate, and communicate evidence about how natural and human-caused changes to habitats or climate can impact populations.
description_plain: Your child can find, understand, and share information about how changes in habitats or climate, caused by nature or humans, affect animal and plant populations.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation based on evidence that the changes in an environment can affect the development of the traits in a population of organisms.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how changes in the environment can change traits in groups of living things.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate, and communicate information about patterns between the offspring of plants, and the offspring of animals (including humans); construct an explanation of how genetic information is passed from one generation to the next.
description_plain: Your child can gather and explain information about how traits are passed from parents to offspring in plants and animals, including humans.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, analyze, and communicate evidence to support an explanation that the gravitational force of Earth on objects is directed toward the planet's center.
description_plain: Your child can collect and explain evidence that Earth's gravity pulls objects toward its center.

subject: Science
description: Develop, revise, and use models based on evidence to construct explanations about the movement of the Earth and Moon within our solar system.
description_plain: Your child can build and improve models to explain how Earth and the Moon move in space.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to determine how and where energy is transferred when objects move.
description_plain: Your child can study and explain how and where energy moves when objects are in motion.

subject: Science
description: Define problems and design solutions pertaining to force and motion.
description_plain: Your child can identify problems related to force and motion and design ways to solve them.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, analyze, and communicate evidence of the effects that balanced and unbalanced forces have on the motion of objects.
description_plain: Your child can collect and explain evidence about how balanced and unbalanced forces change how objects move.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation using evidence to demonstrate that objects can affect other objects even when they are not touching.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how objects can affect each other even when they are not touching.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out investigations to demonstrate that some substances combine to form new substances with different properties and others can be mixed without taking on new properties.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do experiments to show that some substances combine to make new ones with different properties, while others just mix without changing.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to explain that matter of any type can be subdivided into particles too small to see and, in a closed system, if properties change or chemical reactions occur, the amount of matter stays the same.
description_plain: Your child can study data to explain that matter is made of tiny particles, and in a closed space, the amount of matter stays the same even if it changes or reacts.

subject: Science
description: Construct a model that shows the cycling of matter and flow of energy in ecosystems.
description_plain: Your child can build a model to show how matter cycles and energy flows through ecosystems.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to demonstrate the interdependence of organisms and their environment including biotic and abiotic factors.
description_plain: Your child can create and use models to show how living things depend on each other and their environment, including both living and non-living parts.

subject: Science
description: Engage in argument from evidence to support a claim about the factors that cause species to change and how humans can impact those factors.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to argue what causes species to change and how humans affect those causes.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an argument regarding the impact of human activities on the environment and how they positively and negatively affect the competition for energy and resources in ecosystems.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain how human activities affect the environment and change how living things compete for energy and resources.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to show how the tilt of Earth's axis causes variations in the length of the day and gives rise to seasons.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how Earth's tilted axis causes day length to change and creates seasons.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to construct an explanation of how eclipses, moon phases, and tides occur within the Sun-Earth-Moon system.
description_plain: Your child can build and use models to explain how eclipses, moon phases, and tides happen because of the Sun, Earth, and Moon.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to explain how constellations and other night sky patterns appear to move due to Earth's rotation and revolution.
description_plain: Your child can create and use models to explain why constellations and night sky patterns seem to move because Earth spins and moves around the Sun.

subject: Science
description: Use ratios and proportions to analyze and interpret data related to scale, properties, and relationships among objects in our solar system.
description_plain: Your child can use math to study and explain sizes, properties, and relationships of objects in our solar system.

subject: Science
description: Investigate and construct an explanation demonstrating that radiation from the Sun provides energy and is absorbed to warm the Earth's surface and atmosphere.
description_plain: Your child can investigate and explain how sunlight gives energy that warms Earth's surface and air.

subject: Science
description: Analyze how humans use technology to store (potential) and/or use (kinetic) energy.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people use technology to store energy and use it when things move.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to predict how forces act on objects at a distance.
description_plain: Your child can build and use a model to predict how forces can act on objects without touching them.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to represent that matter is made up of smaller particles called atoms.
description_plain: Your child can create and use models to show that matter is made of tiny particles called atoms.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out an investigation to demonstrate that variations in temperature and/or pressure affect changes in state of matter.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do experiments to show how changes in temperature or pressure can change matter from one state to another.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to show that changes in states of matter are caused by different rates of movement of atoms in solids, liquids, and gases (Kinetic Theory).
description_plain: Your child can study data to explain that changes in solids, liquids, and gases happen because atoms move at different speeds.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation for how some plant cells convert light energy into food energy.
description_plain: Your child can explain how some plant cells change light into food energy.

subject: Science
description: Explain how organisms maintain internal stability and evaluate the effect of the external factors on organisms' internal stability.
description_plain: Your child can explain how living things keep their insides stable and how outside factors can affect this balance.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to explain how cells, tissues, and organ systems maintain life (animals).
description_plain: Your child can build and use a model to show how cells, tissues, and organs work together to keep animals alive.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation to demonstrate the relationship between major cell structures and cell functions (plant and animal).
description_plain: Your child can explain how the main parts of plant and animal cells help the cells do their jobs.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate, and communicate information to provide evidence that all living things are made of cells, cells come from existing cells, and cells are the basic structural and functional unit of all living things.
description_plain: Your child can find and share information that shows all living things are made of cells, cells come from other cells, and cells are the basic units of life.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to construct an explanation for how advances in technology has improved weather prediction.
description_plain: Your child can study data to explain how new technology has made weather forecasts better.

subject: Science
description: Construct a model to explain how the distribution of fossils and rocks, continental shapes, and seafloor structures provides evidence of the past plate motions.
description_plain: Your child can build a model to explain how fossils, rock layers, continents, and ocean floors show that Earth's plates have moved in the past.

subject: Science
description: Construct a model that shows the cycling of matter and flow of energy in the atmosphere, hydrosphere, and geosphere.
description_plain: Your child can create a model that shows how matter cycles and energy moves through the air, water, and land.

subject: Science
description: Use non-algebraic mathematics and computational thinking to explain Newton's laws of motion.
description_plain: Your child can use simple math and thinking skills to explain Newton's laws of motion.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out an investigation that can support an evidence-based explanation of how objects on Earth are affected by gravitational force.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to explain how gravity affects objects on Earth.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to predict how forces act on objects at a distance.
description_plain: Your child can build and use a model to predict how forces can act on objects from a distance.

subject: Science
description: Collect and analyze data demonstrating how electromagnetic forces can be attractive or repulsive and can vary in strength.
description_plain: Your child can collect and study data to show how electromagnetic forces can pull objects together or push them apart, and how strong these forces can be.

subject: Science
description: Gather and communicate evidence on how the process of natural selection provides an explanation of how new species can evolve.
description_plain: Your child can gather and share evidence to explain how natural selection helps new species develop over time.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to explain how natural selection may lead to increases and decreases of specific traits in populations over time.
description_plain: Your child can create and use a simple model to show how natural selection causes certain traits in a group of animals or plants to become more or less common over time.

subject: Science
description: Communicate how advancements in technology have furthered the field of genetic research and use evidence to support an argument about the positive and negative effects of genetic research on human lives.
description_plain: Your child can explain how new technology has helped scientists learn more about genetics and use facts to discuss the good and bad effects of genetic research on people.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation of how genetic variations occur in offspring through the inheritance of traits or through mutations.
description_plain: Your child can explain how differences in genes happen in children either by inheriting traits from parents or by changes called mutations.

subject: Science
description: Construct and support an argument about how human consumption of limited resources impacts the biosphere.
description_plain: Your child can make and support a clear argument about how using up limited natural resources affects the environment.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate, and communicate information about data and historical patterns to predict natural hazards and other geological events.
description_plain: Your child can find, check, and share information about past data and patterns to help predict natural disasters and other Earth events.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data about the Earth's geological column to communicate relative ages of rock layers and fossils.
description_plain: Your child can study and explain data about Earth's rock layers to tell which layers and fossils are older or younger.

subject: Science
description: Develop a solution to increase efficiency when transferring energy from one source to another.
description_plain: Your child can come up with a way to make energy transfer from one source to another work better.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use mathematical models to explain wave characteristics and interactions.
description_plain: Your child can create and use math models to explain how waves behave and interact.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation on how energy can be transferred from one energy store to another.
description_plain: Your child can explain how energy moves from one place to another.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and evaluate information regarding how scientists identify substances based on unique physical and chemical properties.

subject: Science

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning. Mathematically proficient students look for and describe regularities as they solve multiple related problems. They formulate conjectures about what they notice and communicate observations with precision. While solving problems, students maintain oversight of the process and continually evaluate the reasonableness of their results. This informs and strengthens their understanding of the structure of mathematics which leads to fluency.
description_plain: Your child notices patterns when solving similar problems, makes guesses about what will happen, and explains their thinking clearly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure. Mathematically proficient students use structure and patterns to assist in making connections among mathematical ideas or concepts when making sense of mathematics. Students recognize and apply general mathematical rules to complex situations. They are able to compose and decompose mathematical ideas and notations into familiar relationships. Mathematically proficient students manage their own progress, stepping back for an overview and shifting perspective when needed.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and structures in math to connect ideas and solve problems more easily.

description: Attend to precision. Mathematically proficient students clearly communicate to others using appropriate mathematical terminology, and craft explanations that convey their reasoning. When making mathematical arguments about a solution, strategy, or conjecture, they describe mathematical relationships and connect their words clearly to their representations. Mathematically proficient students understand meanings of symbols used in mathematics, calculate accurately and efficiently, label quantities appropriately, and record their work clearly and concisely.
description_plain: Your child explains math ideas clearly using correct terms and shows their work carefully and accurately.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically. Mathematically proficient students consider available tools when solving a mathematical problem. They choose tools that are relevant and useful to the problem at hand. Proficient students are sufficiently familiar with tools appropriate for their grade or course to make sound decisions about when each of these tools might be helpful; recognizing both the insight to be gained and their limitations. Students deepen their understanding of mathematical concepts when using tools to visualize, explore, compare, communicate, make and test predictions, and understand the thinking of others.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses the best tools, like calculators or drawings, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics. Mathematically proficient students apply the mathematics they know to solve problems arising in everyday life, society, and the workplace. When given a problem in a contextual situation, they identify the mathematical elements of a situation and create a mathematical model that represents those mathematical elements and the relationships among them. Mathematically proficient students use their model to analyze the relationships and draw conclusions. They interpret their mathematical results in the context of the situation and reflect on whether the results make sense, possibly improving the model if it has not served its purpose.
description_plain: Your child uses math to solve real-life problems by creating models, analyzing them, and checking if the answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others. Mathematically proficient students construct mathematical arguments (explain the reasoning underlying a strategy, solution, or conjecture) using concrete, pictorial, or symbolic referents. Arguments may also rely on definitions, assumptions, previously established results, properties, or structures. Mathematically proficient students make conjectures and build a logical progression of statements to explore the truth of their conjectures. They are able to analyze situations by breaking them into cases, and can recognize and use counterexamples. Mathematically proficient students present their arguments in the form of representations, actions on those representations, and explanations in words (oral or written). Students critique others by affirming or questioning the reasoning of others. They can listen to or read the reasoning of others, decide whether it makes sense, ask questions to clarify or improve the reasoning, and validate or build on it. Mathematically proficient students can communicate their arguments, compare them to others, and reconsider their own arguments in response to the critiques of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking, listens to others’ ideas, asks questions, and improves their own reasoning.

description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively. Mathematically proficient students make sense of quantities and their relationships in problem situations. Students can contextualize and decontextualize problems involving quantitative relationships. They contextualize quantities, operations, and expressions by describing a corresponding situation. They decontextualize a situation by representing it symbolically. As they manipulate the symbols, they can pause as needed to access the meaning of the numbers, the units, and the operations that the symbols represent. Mathematically proficient students know and flexibly use different properties of operations, numbers, and geometric objects and when appropriate they interpret their solution in terms of the context.
description_plain: Your child understands and works with numbers and symbols by connecting them to real situations and back again.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them. Mathematically proficient students explain to themselves the meaning of a problem, look for entry points to begin work on the problem, and plan and choose a solution pathway. While engaging in productive struggle to solve a problem, they continually ask themselves, “Does this make sense?" to monitor and evaluate their progress and change course if necessary. Once they have a solution, they look back at the problem to determine if the solution is reasonable and accurate. Mathematically proficient students check their solutions to problems using different methods, approaches, or representations. They also compare and understand different representations of problems and different solution pathways, both their own and those of others.
description_plain: Your child understands problems, tries different ways to solve them, checks their answers, and learns from their work and others’ ideas.
